summary: Matched Link's aerial attack helper block by adding `ftLk_AttackAir.c/.h`, naming the down-air callbacks and setup routines, wiring previous assembly callsites to the new symbols, and introducing Link-specific data/motion-var types needed by the C. The PR also split Link down-special assembly into its own `ftLk_SpecialLw.c` object and updated data-conversion output across fighter asm files from raw `.4byte` constants to typed `.float`/`.double` directives. There were no PR body comments or review comments in the slice.
what_changed: Named and localized Link down-air callbacks as static helpers `lwOnHit`, `lwOnAnim`, and `lwDoAnimHitCapsule` inside `ftLk_AttackAir.c`.
Replaced old generic Link init header declarations `ftLk_800EB3BC`, `ftLk_800EB42C`, and `ftLk_800EB618` with a dedicated `ftLk_AttackAir.h` interface.
Updated assembly callsites in `ft_0877.s` and `it_27CF.s` to call the newly named `ftLk_AttackAir_*` symbols.
Added `ftLk_DatAttrs` with named down-air fields: `attackairlw_hit_vel_y`, `attackairlw_hit_anim_frame_start`, `attackairlw_hit_anim_frame_end`, and `attackairlw_anim_flags[3]`.
Renamed Link's attribute type usage from `ftLinkAttributes` to `ftLk_DatAttrs` in Link init code and updated the CLink todo comment to reference `ftLk_DatAttrs`.
Added `union ftLk_MotionVars` with `attackair.lw_frame_start` and inserted it into `struct Fighter` at the `fp+2340` motion-vars union.
Changed Link on-load logic to populate `attackairlw_hit_anim_frame_end` from `lbMthp_8001E8F8(ftData_80085E50(fp, 72))` before `PUSH_ATTRS(fp, ftLk_DatAttrs)`.
Moved Link down-special assembly out of `ftLk_Init.s` into a new `ftLk_SpecialLw.c` object, preserving it as MWERKS inline asm rather than fully decompiling it in this PR.
Tightened prototypes for helpers used by the matched code, including `ftColl_8007B064(HSD_GObj*, enum_t)`, `ftParts_80074A4C(HSD_GObj*, enum_t, enum_t)`, and declaration of `it_8029DD58`.
Changed `tools/convert_data.py` input glob from `**/ftPe_*.s` to `**/ft/chara/*.s`, producing many typed `.float` and `.double` replacements in fighter asm data sections.
smart_moves: Kept the matched attack-air code small and focused while moving still-unmatched down-special code to a separate file; this reduced `ftLk_Init.s` without forcing an unrelated full decomp.
Introduced Link-specific dat attributes and motion vars before writing behavior code, avoiding magic offsets for the important down-air timing, velocity, and hitbox flag fields.
Used static local callback names (`lwOnHit`, `lwOnAnim`) for file-private behavior instead of exporting more address-style symbols.
Preserved matching-sensitive unused stack padding under `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` in the decompiled functions, making the matching tactic explicit without polluting non-matching builds.
Updated all known callsites to use semantic names (`ftLk_AttackAir_Enter`, `ftLk_AttackAir_SetupParts`) so future callers no longer depend on anonymous `ftLk_800EB...` identifiers.

Factored repeated hit-capsule restoration into `lwDoAnimHitCapsule`, expressing the three-hitbox loop unrolled as the original required while centralizing the shared `ftColl_8007B064` and `ftColl_8007ABD0` calls.
Used typed asm data directives (`.float`, `.double`) across fighter data to expose constant intent and support decompilation work that depends on rodata interpretation.
decomp_lessons: When matching fighter action code, add the corresponding fighter-specific `dat_attrs` and `mv` union fields first; this lets code use names like `fp->mv.lk.attackair.lw_frame_start` instead of brittle offsets.

A PR can productively combine one matched C file with an object split for adjacent unmatched asm, as long as the build graph and headers clearly reflect which parts are matched and which are still inline asm.
For action callbacks, semantic local names are useful even when exported entry points retain address suffixes for unknown behavior, e.g. `ftLk_AttackAir_800EB3BC` plus static `lwOnHit`/`lwOnAnim`.
If a matched function needs non-source-level stack artifacts, guard them with `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` and leave a clear `@todo Unused stack` marker.
Header cleanup matters for matching: moving declarations from broad init headers into action-specific headers reduces accidental dependencies and makes callsite renames easier to search.
Typed function prototypes for shared helpers can be required by a match; replacing `UNK_PARAMS` with `HSD_GObj*` and `enum_t` arguments documents what the new C actually passes.
Changing data conversion scope can create large asm-data churn; future reviewers should distinguish semantic decomp changes from generated `.float`/`.double` directive normalization.
naming_conventions: `ftLk_AttackAir_*` is used as the module prefix for Link aerial-attack routines.
Entry-point names may combine a semantic module prefix with an address suffix when behavior is still partially unknown, as in `ftLk_AttackAir_800EB3BC`.
Fully understood public routines receive semantic names like `ftLk_AttackAir_Enter` and `ftLk_AttackAir_SetupParts`.
File-local callback helpers use short semantic static names: `lwOnHit`, `lwOnAnim`, `lwDoAnimHitCapsule`.
Link dat attributes are named `ftLk_DatAttrs`, replacing the older `ftLinkAttributes` typedef.
Action-specific dat fields use an `attackairlw_` prefix for Link down-air values.
Link motion vars are exposed through `fp->mv.lk.attackair`, with `lw_frame_start` for the down-air timer.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the matched `ftLk_AttackAir` assembly from `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftLk_Init.s` and added C object files to `obj_files.mk`.
Kept `ftLk_SpecialLw` functions as MWERKS inline `asm void` in a new C file, preserving labels/instructions while separating that code from Link init assembly.
Used `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` dummy stack arrays of 8 and 40 bytes in matched C functions to satisfy stack layout requirements.
Updated assembly call instructions to reference renamed C symbols without changing call flow, e.g. `bl ftLk_AttackAir_Enter` and `bl ftLk_AttackAir_SetupParts`.
Converted raw `.4byte` rodata constants in fighter asm to `.float` and `.double` directives, including common constants like `0.0`, `1.0`, `0.5`, `3.0`, and `4503601774854144.0`.
Adjusted `tools/convert_data.py` to process `**/ft/chara/*.s`, broadening data conversion beyond the previous `**/ftPe_*.s` scope.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review bodies were present in the provided PR slice.
Because there was no human discussion in the slice, reviewer intent and rationale beyond the diff cannot be inferred.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully decompile `ftLk_SpecialLw.c` after this PR's inline-asm split.
Search for later renames or field refinements of `ftLk_DatAttrs` offsets `xC`, `x10`, `x2C`, `x48`, `xBC`, `xC4`, and `xD8`.
Check whether `ftColl_8007B064` and `ftParts_80074A4C` prototypes were further typed after this match.
Look for PRs that reuse `fp->mv.lk.attackair.lw_frame_start` or expand `union ftLk_MotionVars` for other Link actions.
Audit other PRs around `tools/convert_data.py` to see whether broad `**/ft/chara/*.s` conversion caused or fixed additional rodata matching issues.
